summary: End-to-end testing of Freccos MVP (backend + frontend). Backend 18/18 pytest tests pass (100%) covering auth, invite, register/mutual-follow, explore, city recs (top-pick grouping + category filter), recommendation CRUD, trip plans (save/unsave/check/prompt), follow/unfollow, user profile, upload+file retrieval, and Google Places autocomplete. Frontend critical flows work end-to-end: login -> /explore, city detail (friends-strip, category-tabs, recs-list), save toggle, FAB add-recommendation flow, MyProfile with invite-code + Cities/Countries hero + Trips/Bucket tabs, settings sheet -> logout. ONE real UI bug found: the fixed 'Made with Emergent' badge (#emergent-badge, z-index 9999, bottom-right) intercepts pointer events on the bottom-nav items on the right side (nav-trips and nav-profile) at mobile widths (390px). Once the badge is hidden, all four nav buttons work correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: The fixed 'Made with Emergent' badge in public/index.html (position: fixed; bottom:16px; right:16px; z-index:9999) sits on top of the bottom navigation bar and intercepts pointer events on the right-side nav items (nav-trips, nav-profile) at the mobile viewport (max-width: 430px wrapper). Real users on mobile cannot reliably tap Trips or Profile. Confirmed via Playwright: clicks fail with 'emergent-badge intercepts pointer events'. When the badge is hidden via JS, both nav-trips and nav-profile navigate correctly to /trips and /me.

issues: Either raise bottom-nav z-index above 9999, or add bottom padding/safe-area to the nav so it sits above the badge, or reposition the badge (e.g., top-right) on app pages.

action_items: Fix overlay collision between #emergent-badge and bottom nav. Suggested fix: in /app/frontend/src/components/BottomNav.jsx wrapper add `style={{ zIndex: 10000 }}` (or move badge to top-right). Verify nav-trips and nav-profile are tappable at 390px width without force=True.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1215 lines (>700 line guideline) — consider splitting into routers (auth, users, cities, recs, trip_plans, upload, places).
Endpoints use the blocking `requests` library inside async path handlers (storage proxy, Google Places). Under load this blocks the asyncio loop — use httpx.AsyncClient or run in a threadpool.
JWT_SECRET is committed in /app/backend/.env — fine for dev but rotate before production.
/api/upload doesn't constrain content_type beyond startswith('image/'); could accept image/svg+xml which can carry script. Whitelist explicit MIME types matching extensions.
/api/auth/google/session inserts a new user_sessions row on every call without dedup — could accumulate stale rows; add TTL index on user_sessions.expires_at.
register/login error messages reveal whether the email exists ('Looks like you already have an account') — minor info leak, OK for invite-only app but worth noting.
All data-testid attributes are present and stable — good for QA.

success_rate.frontend: 100% functionally with badge hidden; nav-trips/nav-profile blocked by overlay at default page load

test_credentials: Seeded users from /app/memory/test_credentials.md verified working: priya@freccos.com / Demo1234! (invite FRECCOS1), arjun@freccos.com / Demo1234!, sara@freccos.com / Demo1234!.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data — used backend's automatic seeding (Priya/Arjun/Sara + 4 cities + 9 recs). Test artifacts created TEST_-prefixed users and a couple of TEST_ recommendations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite is at /app/backend/tests/. Run: `python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v`. For frontend UI tests, the Emergent badge intercepts bottom-nav right-side items; either fix the z-index in code, or hide #emergent-badge via JS before clicking nav-trips/nav-profile. All other selectors work without force=True.
rca of the issue: Root cause: public/index.html appends `<a id='emergent-badge'>` with inline style z-index:9999 + position:fixed + bottom:16px + right:16px. BottomNav.jsx wrapper (.bottom-nav in index.css) has a lower z-index so the badge sits visually on top of nav-trips/nav-profile at mobile width. Reproduction: open https://social-travel-recs.preview.emergentagent.com/login at 390x844, login as priya, attempt to click [data-testid='nav-trips'] or [data-testid='nav-profile'] — Playwright reports emergent-badge intercepts the click. Mitigation: bump BottomNav container z-index to 10000 (or 10001) OR add bottom padding to push nav up OR reposition the Emergent badge.
